findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the structural grep gate (and the edit-set if needed) to cover `scripts/` — specifically update `scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:152-153` to reference the moved `adapters/jira/acli.py` and `adapters/jira/adf.py` paths, and include `scripts/` in the zero-reference grep sweep.
evidence: Plan Testing: 'grep finds zero references to the old root paths of the moved modules in src/ and tests/'
Plan Scope: no mention of scripts/ directory
scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:153: adf_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('adf', '${_SCRIPTS_DIR}/rebar_reconciler/adf.py')
scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:152: from rebar_reconciler import acli as mod (inside python3 -c inline script)
Prior plan-review cache .rebar/cache/plan-review/dfb9-d2d1-455d-48d9/7d1b5abcf49b3b145f25.json confirms this exact gap was noted in a previous review pass
findings.finding: The plan's structural grep gate and Scope section limit the zero-reference verification to 'src/ and tests/', but scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:153 hardcodes spec_from_file_location('adf', '${_SCRIPTS_DIR}/rebar_reconciler/adf.py') and line 152 uses 'from rebar_reconciler import acli as mod' — both root-path references that break after the move. This is an empirically-checkable codebase exclusion that the tools refute; the gate would spuriously pass while these scripts silently break.
scenarios: After the move acli.py and adf.py no longer exist at rebar_reconciler/acli.py / rebar_reconciler/adf.py; the e2e pressure-test probe fails immediately with FileNotFoundError / ImportError on any run post-deploy.
The structural grep gate passes (it only checks src/ and tests/) while the broken script goes undetected until an ops/pressure-test run.
findings.suggested_fix: In e2e_field_validation_probe.sh, change line 152's 'from rebar_reconciler import acli' to import from the new adapter path (or adjust RECONCILER_DIR + path constant), and update line 153's hardcoded path from '${_SCRIPTS_DIR}/rebar_reconciler/adf.py' to '${_SCRIPTS_DIR}/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/adf.py'. Extend the Structural gate grep command to include scripts/: `grep -r 'rebar_reconciler/acli.py\|rebar_reconciler/adf.py\|rebar_reconciler/comment_limits' src/ tests/ scripts/`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete, anchor-safe grep command to the structural-gate AC, e.g. `grep -rE 'rebar_reconciler\.(acli|adf|outbound_fields|comment_limits)' src/ tests/ | grep -v '_load_acli' | grep -c '' && echo FAIL || echo PASS` (or equivalent), so the gate cannot false-positive on `_load_acli` matches or omit a cardinality assertion.
evidence: 'grep finds zero references to the old root paths of the moved modules in src/ and tests/ (excluding the path-agnostic _load_acli patch.object sites)' — no grep command given
Defect taxonomy #1: substring false-positive — `grep rebar_reconciler.acli` matches `_load_acli`
Defect taxonomy #5: no cardinality assertion (`-c 0` or `wc -l` check) specified
findings.finding: The structural-gate grep command is prose-only — no concrete grep invocation with word-boundary anchors or the required exclusion pattern is specified. Per defect taxonomy #1 (substring false-positive) and #5 (missing cardinality), `grep rebar_reconciler.acli` would match the path-agnostic `_load_acli` strings the plan explicitly excludes, producing false positives; and no cardinality assertion (`-c 0` / `|| echo FAIL`) is specified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('zero hits ... excluding the path-agnostic `_load_acli` patches') and Testing — 'Structural gate' paragraph
scenarios: An executor writes `grep -r 'rebar_reconciler.acli' src/ tests/` which hits ~23 `_load_acli` sites and either falsely fails the gate or excludes too broadly and misses real strays.
A partial migration leaves one old import but the un-anchored grep misses it, producing a false-green gate.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the prose description with a runnable command block:
```
grep -rE 'rebar_reconciler\.(acli[^_]|acli_cli_ops|acli_graph|acli_rest|acli_subprocess|adf|outbound_fields|comment_limits)' src/ tests/ \
  | grep -v 'patch\.object.*_load_acli' \
  | wc -l | xargs -I{} test {} -eq 0 && echo 'GATE PASS' || echo 'GATE FAIL'
```
This anchors the pattern to whole module names, explicitly excludes the `patch.object(_load_acli)` sites, and asserts a count of zero.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete pytest invocation (e.g. `pytest tests/loader/ -k 'test_adf_loads_from_adapter_path or test_root_module_load_unaffected' -v`) or a named test file to AC2 so the loader test is independently verifiable, not only subsumed by the full suite.
evidence: 'dynamically loading adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits through the reconciler entry point succeeds at the new location (staging the move without the loader edit fails RED)' — no command given
AC2: 'a root-module dynamic load still succeeds (loader test + non-moved-consumer regression)' — references test names but no invocation
findings.finding: The 'Loader test' AC requires that 'dynamically loading adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits through the reconciler entry point succeeds at the new location' but no concrete proving command (test name, pytest invocation, or script) is specified — 'make test' as a whole-suite command does not isolate this criterion and cannot confirm the loader path specifically exercised it.
findings.location: Testing — 'Loader test' paragraph; Acceptance Criteria — AC2
scenarios: The full suite is green but the loader-specific regression was never written; AC2 passes vacuously because the old loader path happens to still resolve (e.g. a transitive import masks the failure).
An executor cannot confirm which test exercises the loader path without hunting through the test suite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the plan title to accurately name the actual migration targets (e.g. 'migrate ~18 path constants + ~18 dotted imports atomically') so it matches the body's corrected category characterization.
evidence: Title: 'migrate ~29 patch strings atomically'
Body: 'the first draft's "~29 mock.patch strings" was wrong; there are zero mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") string patches'
Actual migration targets: '~18 files hardcode importlib.util.spec_from_file_location…path constants' (category 2) and '~18 files use direct dotted imports' (category 3)
findings.finding: The plan title names 'migrate ~29 patch strings' as the migration mechanism, but the plan body explicitly states there are zero mock.patch string patches — the actual category-2/3 migration targets are ~18 path constants and ~18 dotted imports. An implementer reading only the title (e.g. in a ticket or PR description) will look for patch-string rewrites and not find them, potentially missing the actual work (path constants in importlib.util.spec_from_file_location calls and direct dotted import rewrites).
findings.location: Title: '…migrate ~29 patch strings atomically' vs. Testing section: 'there are zero mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acli…") string patches'
scenarios: An implementer or reviewer scoping the PR from the title searches for mock.patch string rewrites, finds none, and incorrectly concludes the category-2 path-constant rewrites were already done or are out of scope — leaving ~18 importlib path constants unrewritten and the structural gate silently broken.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '~29 patch strings' in the title with '~36 path-constant/dotted-import references' (or similar) to match the body's two-category breakdown.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the structural gate (or add an AC bullet) to confirm that `docs/`, `AGENTS.md`, and any top-level `README` contain no stale references to the eight moved modules at their old root paths — and update any that do.
evidence: '**Structural gate:** `grep` finds **zero** references to the old root paths of the moved modules in `src/` and `tests/`'

ABSENCE: no mention of scanning `docs/`, `AGENTS.md`, `README`, or any other developer-facing documentation for stale path references to `acli.py`, `adf.py`, `outbound_fields.py`, `comment_limits.py`, etc. at the package root
findings.finding: The structural gate's grep sweep is scoped to `src/` and `tests/` only, leaving any references to the old root-path module names in developer-facing docs (e.g., AGENTS.md, README, any architecture docs under `docs/`) unverified and potentially stranded after the move.
findings.location: Testing section — 'Structural gate: grep finds zero references…in `src/` and `tests/`' and Scope section
scenarios: A developer reads AGENTS.md and follows a stale path reference to a module that no longer exists at the root, wasting time debugging an import error or misunderstanding the architecture.
CI passes the structural gate (src+tests clean) while AGENTS.md still documents the old layout, creating drift between docs and reality.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: 'grep for old root-path module names (`acli`, `adf`, `outbound_fields`, `comment_limits`) in `docs/`, `AGENTS.md`, and top-level `README*` returns zero hits (or any hits are updated to reflect the new `adapters/jira/` location).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fourth test-surface category — 'source-path structural assertions using `.read_text()` or `.open()` on moved module paths' — covering `tests/interfaces/store/test_commits_on_ticket.py:119`, and include it in the migration set with the path updated to `adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py`.
evidence: tests/interfaces/store/test_commits_on_ticket.py:115-119: fields_mod = Path(rebar.__file__).resolve().parent / '_engine' / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'outbound_fields.py'; src = fields_mod.read_text(encoding='utf-8')

Plan: 'Three distinct categories: (1) patch.object … (2) ~18 files hardcode importlib.util.spec_from_file_location … (3) ~18 files use direct dotted imports' — no category covers read_text path references to moved source files
findings.finding: tests/interfaces/store/test_commits_on_ticket.py:119 constructs a filesystem path to outbound_fields.py at the old root location and calls .read_text() on it as a source-text structural assertion. This is neither a mock.patch (category 1), a spec_from_file_location import (category 2), nor a dotted import (category 3) — it falls outside all three enumerated test categories in the plan. After the move, the file no longer exists at that path and the test will raise FileNotFoundError (or read a re-export shim if one were added, but none is permitted).
findings.location: Testing section — three-category test taxonomy; Approach — deduped category-2/3 migration set
scenarios: After outbound_fields.py is moved to adapters/jira/, fields_mod.read_text() raises FileNotFoundError, failing the interface-level test with no coverage by any of the three migration categories.

The structural gate grep pattern ('grep for old root paths') should catch this string literal, but the plan's AC does not call out this test file or category as requiring a rewrite, so it may be overlooked during implementation.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Testing section, add: 'Category 4: ~1 file uses Path(...) / "outbound_fields.py" with .read_text() for structural assertion — update path to adapters/jira/outbound_fields.py.' Update test_commits_on_ticket.py line 119 accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `apply_handlers.py` explicitly to the leaf's repoint edit-set: since S4 (parent AC7) placed `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import AssigneeNotFoundError` there and S5 moves `acli_subprocess.py`, the leaf must update this import to `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.acli_subprocess import AssigneeNotFoundError` and list `apply_handlers.py` as an explicit repoint site alongside `jira_fields.py:18`, `inbound_fields.py:36`, and `acli_cli_ops.py:36`.
evidence: Parent S4 AC7: 'apply_handlers.py:181 catches AssigneeNotFoundError via a direct `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import AssigneeNotFoundError` (not ctx.acli.…)'
Leaf moves `acli_subprocess.py` as one of the eight modules: 'Physically move the eight modules into src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/'
Leaf explicit repoint enumeration lists jira_fields.py:18, inbound_fields.py:36, acli_cli_ops.py:36, acli.py:76 — `apply_handlers.py` is absent
Leaf AC3: 'Every surviving dotted/sibling reference is repointed … verified by grep for the old targets returning zero hits' — implicit coverage only, no explicit edit-set entry
findings.finding: The leaf's explicit edit-set omits `apply_handlers.py` even though parent S4's AC7 deliberately places `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import AssigneeNotFoundError` there, and the leaf moves `acli_subprocess.py` to `adapters/jira/` — leaving that S4-delivered import stale. The leaf's Scope section enumerates specific repoint sites (`jira_fields.py:18`, `inbound_fields.py:36`, `acli_cli_ops.py:36`, `acli.py:76`, `acli_cli_ops.py:36`) but never mentions `apply_handlers.py`. The verification grep gate would eventually surface it, but an implementer following the leaf's stated edit-set as written would not proactively update this core-module import, risking a broken assignee soft-fail path post-move.
findings.location: Scope section / 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint' enumeration; also AC line 3 ('Every surviving dotted/sibling reference is repointed')
scenarios: Developer implements the leaf following the explicit edit-set, moves acli_subprocess.py, misses apply_handlers.py, ships a broken assignee-soft-fail code path that crashes at the `except AssigneeNotFoundError` site because the import resolves the old (now absent) root path.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Surviving intra-adapter / non-core references to repoint' list, add: '`apply_handlers.py:181` — `from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import AssigneeNotFoundError` (placed by S4 AC7; acli_subprocess moves with the acli* cluster) → `from rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.acli_subprocess import AssigneeNotFoundError`.' Also add it to AC3's explicit verification list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out / rollback statement to the Approach section — e.g. 'If the suite is RED after merge, revert the entire commit via `git revert <sha>`; because no shims exist at old paths, a partial rollback is not safe' — so the reversibility mechanism is documented.
evidence: Approach: 'One atomic change (the constraints forbid a partial move).'
AC1: 'no source file remains at their old root paths and no re-export shim exists there'
ABSENCE: no revert procedure, no git-revert mention, no expand-contract fallback, no 'if suite is RED after the move, do X' guidance
findings.finding: The plan performs a destructive, irreversible operation — removing eight modules from their existing root paths with no re-export shims (explicitly per ADR Decision 4) — but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan. The plan says 'atomic' but atomicity describes how the change lands, not what happens if the suite is RED after landing or if a partial failure occurs in a CI pipeline that applies changes incrementally.
findings.location: Approach section, Acceptance Criteria (all lines), Testing section
scenarios: Suite is RED after the atomic commit lands on main; no back-out procedure is specified so the team must improvise a revert or patch under pressure.
A CI pipeline applies the physical move but fails before repointing all references; the repo is left in a broken intermediate state with no documented recovery path.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Approach: 'Rollback: because no re-export shims exist, a partial back-out is unsafe. If post-merge CI is RED, revert the full commit atomically (`git revert <sha>`) and re-land after fixing. Do not cherry-pick partial fixes.'

grep: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py:24-25: from rebar_reconciler import acli as acli_mod` + `acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`

Plan's category-3 description: '~18 files use direct dotted imports from rebar_reconciler.acli… / .adf / .outbound_fields → must be rewritten to adapters.jira.*'
findings.finding: Several test files contain direct dotted imports of moved modules that are not listed in the plan's category-3 test migration set: `test_idea_status_translation.py:17` (`from rebar_reconciler import outbound_fields`), `test_comment_pagination.py:26-27` (`from rebar_reconciler import acli as acli_mod` + `acli_cli_ops, acli_subprocess`), `test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py:24-25` (same pattern), `test_config_file_wiring.py:21` (`from rebar_reconciler import acli_subprocess`), `test_ev3c_env_renames.py:10` (same), and `test_config_jira_wiring.py:20` (`from rebar_reconciler.acli_subprocess import resolve_jira_settings`).
scenarios: These tests will fail with ImportError/ModuleNotFoundError after the move if their dotted imports are not rewritten to the adapters.jira.* paths.
The zero-hits grep gate (AC item 3) will not catch these if they use package-level `from rebar_reconciler import <module>` syntax rather than `from rebar_reconciler.<module> import`.

tests/scripts/conftest.py:37: ACLI_PATH = REPO_ROOT / 'src' / 'rebar' / '_engine' / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'acli.py'  (parallel inline constant showing both exist)
Plan says 'category-2: ~18 files hardcode importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(…, acli.py-style path constants naming a moved module → must be rewritten' — does not mention the shared helper module
findings.finding: tests/_engine_path.py:26 defines a shared acli_path() helper that returns engine_dir() / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'acli.py' (the old root path). This is a centralised utility imported by multiple consumers (tests/scripts/conftest.py:37 and others); the plan's category-2 enumeration covers inline per-file path constants but does not mention this shared helper. After the move, every consumer of acli_path() silently points at a non-existent file, and updating only inline constants leaves the shared helper broken.
findings.location: Approach / Testing / AC §3 — category-2 path-constant migration set
scenarios: If tests/_engine_path.py is not updated, every test that calls acli_path() (e.g. tests/scripts/conftest.py consumers) will load the module from the old non-existent path and raise FileNotFoundError at test time, or silently fail spec creation.

The plan's structural grep gate (grep for old root paths in tests/) would catch this IF the grep pattern includes python identifiers and function return values, but the plan's stated grep pattern targets string literals, which may miss the Path expression in acli_path().
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach and Scope sections, add `tests/_engine_path.py:acli_path()` to the category-2 path-constant list. Update the function to return `engine_dir() / 'rebar_reconciler' / 'adapters' / 'jira' / 'acli.py'`. Extend the structural grep gate to cover function return expressions, not only string literals.

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three the same way' for adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits, but __main__.py currently invokes `_load_sibling_keyed` only for `mode.py` and `_advisory_lock.py` — none of the three location-pinned modules are loaded through `__main__._load_sibling_keyed`. Grep confirms zero calls to `_load_sibling_keyed` or `spec_from_file_location` in __main__.py for these modules.
findings.location: Edit-set section / Loader paragraph: 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three the same way'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the clause 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three the same way' since grep confirms no such sites exist in __main__.py for these three modules. The loader update is confined to the lazy_load() calls in the consumer modules themselves.

findings.finding: The plan states 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three [adf/outbound_fields/comment_limits] the same way.' But `__main__.py` contains no `_load_sibling_keyed` call sites for `adf`, `outbound_fields`, or `comment_limits` — the function is only called for `_advisory_lock.py` (line 534) and `mode.py` (line 516). This claim is factually incorrect against the current codebase and could mislead the implementer into modifying `__main__` unnecessarily or waste time searching for non-existent call sites.
findings.location: Approach section: 'Update __main__._load_sibling_keyed and the importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...) sites for these three the same way'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three the same way' with 'The three moved location-pinned modules are loaded via `lazy_load(key, filename)` inside `outbound_comments.py`, `outbound_fields.py`, `inbound_translate.py`, `reconcile_check.py`, and `inbound_fields.py` (S4 may eliminate some of these); update those call sites to pass `"adapters/jira/adf.py"` etc. `__main__.py` has no such call sites and requires no change for these three modules.'

findings.finding: The plan claims 'Update `__main__._load_sibling_keyed` and the `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(...)` sites for these three [location-pinned modules] the same way', but `__main__.py` contains zero references to `adf`, `outbound_fields`, or `comment_limits` — the asserted update sites do not exist.
